company_name: United States Natural Gas Fund, LP
turnaround_reason: Not a stock — this is a natural gas commodity-tracking ETF (LP structure). Surfaces in the screen because natural gas spot prices have been in multi-year decline, making UNG look like a deeply-beat-down recovery candidate. But UNG also suffers from futures-curve contango decay independent of spot direction, making it a poor long-term hold even if natural gas does recover.
bullish_points: N/A — commodity ETF, not an operating business
bearish_points: Natural gas futures curve has been in chronic contango — UNG decays from rolling losses even when spot prices are flat
UNG is well-known among commodity ETF investors as one of the worst long-term holds due to roll yield
Macro natural gas thesis (LNG export growth, AI data-center power demand) is real but the right instrument is producer equities (EQT, AR, RRC) — not UNG itself
Even if natural gas spot doubles, UNG might only return 30-50% due to decay
catalyst: Sustained natural gas spot price recovery + curve backwardation (a multi-factor macro setup)
suggested_trade: Skip. If you want bullish natural gas exposure, use producer equities (EQT, AR, RRC, CTRA) or futures directly. UNG is the wrong instrument. Action item — same filter recommendation as GLL: exclude ETFs.
key_risk: N/A — not an operating company; structural decay regardless of natural gas direction
would_skip_if: Always — ETFs should never pass an equity-turnaround screen
